target.title: fix(feishu): respect skipReplyToInMessages in streaming card start
target.body:
## Summary

When `blockStreaming` is enabled (default), the Feishu streaming card delivery path started the streaming session with the raw `replyToMessageId` parameter, bypassing the `skipReplyToInMessages` filter that already existed for regular text/card message replies.

This caused Feishu DM (direct message) replies to incorrectly show a "Reply to [User]" label, since the streaming card was sent as a reply rather than a top-level message.

The fix changes `replyToMessageId` to `sendReplyToMessageId` in the `streaming.start()` call, so the `skipReplyToInMessages` flag is respected in the streaming path as well.

Fixes #94922

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu streaming card reply mode in DM chats

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Linux, Node v24.16.0
- **Test suite**: `pnpm test -- extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts` — 82/82 tests pass

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
pnpm test -- extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  82 passed (82)
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All 82 existing tests continue to pass. The `streaming.start()` call now uses `sendReplyToMessageId` instead of the raw `replyToMessageId`, ensuring the `skipReplyToInMessages` flag is respected for DM conversations.

**What was not tested**: Live end-to-end test with actual Feishu API (requires Feishu bot credentials). The fix is verified through unit tests and code review.

## Tests and validation

| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ | 82/82 passed |

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)
- DM Feishu replies will no longer show "Reply to [User]" label
- Group/topic behavior is unchanged

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?
- Streaming card delivery in group/topic scenarios where `skipReplyToInMessages` should be `false`

How is that risk mitigated?
- The change uses the existing `sendReplyToMessageId` variable which is already correctly computed from `skipReplyToInMessages`
- All 82 existing tests pass, including tests that verify streaming card behavior in both DM and group scenarios
- The `sendReplyToMessageId` variable is defined on line 173 of the same file and used by all other delivery paths

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review
